reasoning: The Lean statement `IsOpen U ↔ ∀ x ∈ U, ¬ ClusterPt x (𝓟 Uᶜ)` matches the informal claim. By `clusterPt_principal_iff` in Mathlib, `ClusterPt x (𝓟 Uᶜ)` means every neighborhood of x meets Uᶜ, i.e., x ∈ closure(Uᶜ). So the Lean statement says: U is open iff no x ∈ U is in the closure of Uᶜ, which is exactly equivalent to "no point of U is a limit of Uᶜ". Since the quantifier restricts to x ∈ U (so x ∉ Uᶜ), cluster point and accumulation point coincide, so either reading of "limit" (Pugh's accumulation point or adherent/cluster point) gives the same statement. Generalizing from Pugh's metric-space context to `TopologicalSpace` is the natural abstract setting; the mathematical content is identical.
